exemplar.title: Python programme to manage car and house rental business

detailedExemplarData.title: Python programme to manage car and house rental business

strengths: Detailed evidence of iterative consultation via full interview transcripts
Clear identification of client and appropriate scenario
Specific and measurable success criteria addressing core functionality
weaknesses: Scenario description lacks depth on operational context and client pain points
Rationale omits explicit comparisons to alternative platforms and deeper feature linkage
Success criteria miss aspects such as security, portability, and maintainability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The planning section shows strong consultation and measurable success criteria, placing it solidly in the 3â4 band. However, the scenario and product rationale lack the depth and justification needed for the top band. A score of 4 reflects these strengths while acknowledging areas for improvement.

strengths: Moderate algorithmic complexity (sorting toggle, conflict detection) demonstrating ingenuity
Effective and varied use of external libraries (Tkinter, tkcalendar, ttk) integrated appropriately
Clear code explanations with annotated screenshots linking techniques to project needs
weaknesses: No external sources or tutorials cited, lacking formal documentation of origins
Opportunities remain to optimize or refactor file I/O for configurability
Complexity is solid but does not reach the very highest SL expectations for innovativeness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Development shows solid moderate complexity, effective tool usage, and clear technique explanations but is held back by missing source attribution and room for deeper innovation. This warrants a strong midâtoâhigh band score of 7 out of 12.

strengths: Comprehensive, detailed task record covering the full project lifecycle with timestamps and criterion links
Wellâdeveloped design overview including wireframes, flowchart, and input/output tables
Test plan covers normal and extreme cases with expected outcomes for most tests
weaknesses: Design mock-ups lack data dictionary or class diagram for full technical clarity
Some test cases have incomplete or vague expected outcomes (e.g., search bar)
Traceability between success criteria and test cases could be improved
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The solution overview is thorough, with a bestâinâband task record and strong design artifacts, meriting a high midâband score. Minor omissions in design documentation and testâcase detail mean it falls short of the absolute top band, justifying a score of 4.

strengths: Product functions reliably in add/edit/delete, search, conflict checks, and persistence as demonstrated
Modular code structure supports straightforward addition of new fields or database backâend
weaknesses: Hard-coded file paths impede configurability and extensibility
Some extension tasks (e.g., UI enhancements) would require nonâtrivial refactoring
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The product clearly functions well, earning top marks for functionality, but extensibility is only straightforward, not exemplary. A combined score of 2 out of 4 reflects full functionality with limited ease of modification.

strengths: Systematic evaluation against each success criterion with met/partially met judgments
Comprehensive client feedback transcripts guiding refinements
Realistic future recommendations directly linked to client comments
weaknesses: Depth of quantitative testing evidence in evaluation is light
Lack of external adviser feedback limits perspective
Recommendations are wellâjustified but lack prioritization and cost/effort estimates
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation revisits all success criteria and includes strong client feedback with practical recommendations, fitting the 3â4 band. The absence of adviser input and lighter testing evidence constrain the score to 3.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student provides input/output tables, two sets of wireframes, a first prototype mock-up and a full flow-chart. The artefacts show structure, navigation and data handling, demonstrating clear planning. Minor omissions (no data dictionary or class diagram) prevent a perfect 6.

The rationale states why a Python/Tkinter solution was chosen (familiarity, large community, ease of error-fixing) and links these points to reliability for the client. However, it stops short of weighing alternatives or explicitly linking each feature of the chosen platform to the clientâs needs in depth. Hence it is more than just âidentifiedâ but not âfully justified,â meriting the mid-band.

Effective use of Tkinter, ttk, tkcalendar, messagebox, file I/O and datetime demonstrates appropriate, varied and integrated tool usage. The implementation shows proficiency but not the breadth or optimisation expected for the very top of the course, so 7/8 is fitting.

A test plan is present with general function tests and several abnormal/extreme input tables. Expected outcomes are supplied for most, though some are incomplete or vague (e.g., search bar row split). The plan is therefore âbasic outlineâ leaning to detailed, justifying the top of the 3-4 band.

The student groups improvements into âminorâ and âmajorâ, each linked to client comments and offering practical implementation routes (asterisks, database migration, colour tagging). The suggestions are realistic and justified, though not costed or prioritised in depth, so 5/6 is appropriate.

The write-up explains each advanced technique with annotated code screenshots, but external sources or tutorials are not cited and there is no bibliography. Techniques are explained, giving more than âlimitedâ documentation, but lack of full attribution restricts the mark to the upper end of the 1-4 band.

The task table spans the full project life-cycle, contains estimated time, actual completion dates and criterion links for 24 tasks, clearly evidencing progress. Detail and completeness place it firmly in the highest band.

The product includes GUI creation, dynamic treeview sorting, search, unique IDs, conflict detection, text-file persistence and multi-type event handlingâdemonstrating multiple inter-acting algorithms of moderate complexity. The ingenuity is clear but still within typical SL scope; hence mid-high marks in the 5-8 band.

Seven success criteria are listed, most of which are specific and testable (e.g., 50 % faster input, double-booking prevention). They address usability, data integrity and performance. While coverage of portability, security or maintainability is missing, the range is still âcomprehensiveâ for the core problem, so the top of the 3-4 band is appropriate.

Two detailed interview transcripts (third and fourth) provide rich, specific feedback on GUI clarity, search bar, additional columns etc., demonstrating comprehensive dialogue. Minor absence of adviser feedback keeps the score at 5 rather than 6.

Modular functions (add car, add event, load/save files) mean new fields or database back-end could be integrated without major rewrites, as noted in recommendations. Some hard-coded file structures remain, so extension is âstraightforwardâ but not exemplary, meriting 3.

The student defines a real business context, identifies the individual owner, and embeds two full interview transcripts showing iterative consultation (Appendices AâC). The dialogue captures both requirements and subsequent refinements (search bar, split event types), demonstrating sustained evidence rather than a single contact. This matches the descriptor of âdetailed evidence of consultation,â although the description of the business itself remains fairly succinct, so the very top of the band (6) is not fully warranted.

Narrative and screenshots indicate the programme runs, supports add/edit/delete, search, conflict checks and note saving. The client confirms it works and timing evidence supports efficiency. This aligns with âfunctions wellâ, so full marks in the top band for this criterion.

Each original success criterion is revisited with a met/partially met judgement and brief justification. Although depth of testing evidence is light, the coverage is complete, satisfying full evaluation within the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The scenario briefly outlines the clientâs rental business but lacks depth on operational scale and context. Expanding the description to include client pain points, business size, and target users will strengthen the scenario.

The rationale identifies Python/Tkinter familiarity but omits evaluation of alternatives or explicit linkage of platform features to client requirements. Consider comparing other frameworks and justifying each choice in relation to client needs.
The success criteria are mostly specific (e.g., 50% faster input) and measurable, addressing usability and performance. This fosters clear evaluation benchmarks.

While success criteria cover core functionality, they omit maintainability, security, and portability aspects. Including criteria for data security and future maintainability would provide a more comprehensive evaluation framework.
The task record is detailed and complete, showing planned actions, outcomes, timestamps, and criterion links. This provides a clear development timeline and evidence of systematic planning.
Continuation of the task log maintains consistency and depth across criteria AâE, reinforcing a comprehensive development process record.
The Input/Output table thoroughly maps each field to data types, valid examples, and error cases, supporting a clear design specification.
The âWrong type of inputâ entries are marked as âââ, which lacks guidance on handling errors. Specify expected error messages or input validation rules to clarify system behavior.
The table of outputs comprehensively lists warnings and info messages, detailing scenarios and expected messages, which strengthens the test planning.
The first prototype wireframe demonstrates initial layout concepts but has inconsistent alignment and sparse annotation, reducing clarity. Consider refining grid alignment and labeling controls.
The second wireframe is annotated with user interactions and sample values, clearly showing field purpose and flow. This demonstrates effective design iteration.
The Test Plan section covers main window functionality and special cases. Including a clear mapping between success criteria and test cases would further improve traceability.
Some test cases lack detailed expected outcomes (e.g., search bar entry). Defining precise expected results for each test improves verification rigour.
The test plan includes sorting, filtering, and button functionality, demonstrating thorough coverage of dynamic behaviors.
Extreme input testing is well considered (e.g., long names, distant dates), showing foresight in handling boundary conditions.
The list of techniques identifies a wide range of advanced methods (calendars, file handling, event IDs), illustrating the projectâs technical depth.
The explanation of external libraries highlights their roles in UI, date handling, and persistence, linking choices to program reliability.
The discussion of the Calendar widget articulates usability benefits and error reduction, showing appropriate use of third-party tools.
Input validation via messagebox.showerror for required fields demonstrates robust error handling and prevents incomplete data entry.
The try-except sorting algorithm handles both numeric and text columns, toggling sort direction. This reflects moderate algorithmic ingenuity.
The conflict detection algorithm converts dates, checks overlaps, and warns the user, showcasing a well-structured logical check to prevent double bookings.
Event handling is clearly described, linking GUI actions to function calls and demonstrating dynamic interaction design.
Persistent file I/O with automatic backups and dynamic load/save routines enhances data integrity and demonstrates effective file handling.
Unique event IDs combine timestamp and count to avoid collisions, showing thoughtful design for data consistency and traceability.
Button command binding is consistent and enhances usability with clear one-click actions for core functions.
The product functions well, meeting most success criteria (add/edit/delete, conflict checks, data persistence) as confirmed by user testing.
The modular code structure supports straightforward extension (e.g., adding new fields or migrating to a database) but some file paths remain hard-coded. Refactor I/O for configurability.
The evaluation revisits each success criterion with clear âmetâ or âpartially metâ judgements, demonstrating systematic reflection.
Rich client interview transcripts are included, providing comprehensive qualitative feedback to guide refinements.
Future recommendations are realistic and directly linked to client feedback, with clear implementation strategies for minor and major changes.
